rankings.writeup: Team Matt sits atop the standings for the first time this season after a gutsy 64-62 win over Sean. Matt Paik dropped 35 points (5-for-10 from three, 10-for-12 from the line) in another superstar performance, but the real story was David Kim — 20 points on a scorching 10-for-13 from the field. That's the kind of secondary scoring this team has been begging for all season. Yes, Sean Lee was absent, but you can only beat what's in front of you. At 4-2 with the league's best win percentage (.667), Matt has turned a shaky 3-2 stretch into a legitimate title contender. The PJo loss from last week already feels like ancient history.

Team Nathan had the bye this week, but the landscape shifted in their favor. Sean's loss to Matt means Nathan and Sean now have identical 3-2-1 records, and Nathan still boasts the league's most complete player in Nathan Paik (26.8/10.8/3.5). The upcoming matchup against Matt is the biggest game of the season — a win would tie them for the best record in the league and lock up Nathan's MVP case. A loss would drop them to 3-3-1 and could end the race entirely. Everything rides on Week 7.

A rough week for Team Sean — a 64-62 loss to Matt without Sean Lee in the lineup exposed the team's depth problem. Christian Kim was magnificent as always (28 points, 21 rebounds, 2 blocks), but he can't do it alone. Justin Park stepped up with 15 points (5-for-14 from three) and Ethan Lee contributed 3 assists, 2 steals, and a block on defense, but went 0-for-9 from the field. The question looming over this team is simple: how often will Sean Lee be available? When he's there, they're arguably the best team in the league. When he's not, they're vulnerable. With two games in Week 7 (PJo and Aron), they need him back to avoid falling further.

Team Aron snapped their two-game skid with a convincing 65-52 win over PJo. Aron Kim was a stat-sheet stuffer with 11 points, 17 rebounds (6 offensive), 7 assists, 2 steals, and a block — a near triple-double that showed his full skillset beyond scoring. Jonathan Chung led the way with 22 points on 8-for-15 shooting (3-for-7 from three), and Jon Han added 12 points and 11 rebounds with 7 offensive boards. The team dominated the glass and the result was never in doubt. At 3-3, they're back to .500, but the inconsistency remains concerning — they look dominant one week and flat the next. A strong showing against Sean in Week 7 could push them back into contention.

The upset over Matt last week turned out to be lightning in a bottle. Team PJo fell 52-65 to Aron, unable to keep up with Aron's physical dominance on the boards. Justin Ho did everything he could with 24 points (4-for-10 from three, 6-for-7 from the line), but PJo himself struggled (6-for-16, 1-for-5 from the line) and committed 5 turnovers. The team shot just 18-for-55 (32.7%) from the field and was outrebounded badly. At 1-5, the playoff picture is bleak. The one silver lining: Isaac Seong grabbed 5 offensive rebounds and PJun had 3 steals, showing some fight on the hustle plays.
